Off Calendar Apr-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas A. Steig of PRINDLE, AMARO, GOETZ, HILLYARD, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant / moving party Trane U.S. Inc.
Kelly McMeekin of NAPOLI, BERN, RIPKA & SHKOLNIK, LLP (310-331-8224), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Lavon Hoelmer, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TRANE U.S. INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Trane U.S. Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Anderson's deposition and declaration, American Standard Inc.'s responses to master interrogatories and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation board) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Mr. Olson and Mr. Uhl are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Anderson's declaration are sustained as to any statements in the declaration about asbestos and all other objections are overruled. Defendant's objections to American Standard's interrogatory responses are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. An order after hearing and 3 orders re: defendant's evidentiary objections were signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT GROENIGER & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Groeniger & Company's demurrer: Defendant's general demurrer to plaintiffs' entire complaint based on the doctrine of res judicata is overruled. All the requirements to apply that doctrine are not met here, namely that there be a final judgment in the prior action. Defendant's general demurrer to plaintiffs' entire complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara L. Blakely of HG LITIGATION SERVICES, CSR #5806 (503/TLJ)
